overview: Saga Moon Red Stompy combines Blood Moon and Urza's Saga to create a prison strategy that freezes the Saga at two lore counters, generating infinite Construct tokens. The deck leverages acceleration, graveyard hate, and equip artifacts like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to apply early pressure and lock down opponents. It thrives on disrupting mana and graveyard-dependent strategies while pushing damage through lifelink and equipped tokens.
win_conditions: Freezing Urza's Saga with Blood Moon to generate infinite Construct tokens
Equipping tokens with Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Lavaspur Boots to push damage
Using Chalice of the Void to lock opponent's key spells or lands
Lifelink from Shadowspear to win the life total race

keep_criteria: Hands that can play a threat or disruption on turn 1 or 2
Hands containing acceleration and The One Ring to recover card disadvantage
Hands with graveyard hate when playing first against Dimir Reanimator
Keep hands with 1-2 lands, Chrome Mox, and at least one of Chalice of the Void, a turn-1/2 threat, or discard.
Prioritize hands that can chain Mox into a threat or Chalice into a disruption piece.
mulligan_criteria: Hands that are too slow to return fire or lack turn 1/2 impact
Hands lacking sufficient mana resources to cast key cards
Hands vulnerable to Daze or Wasteland without backup plans
Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no Mox or threats, or if you lack any way to play a spell on turn 1 or 2.

examples.reason: Too slow to return fire and vulnerable to Daze unless playing first against Reanimator
Extremely risky, relies heavily on drawing mana and vulnerable to Wasteland
Can sequence to play Fable and equip token on turn 2, or play Fable turn 1 if Chalice is inefficient
Can play Magus on turn 1 against certain decks if able to draw additional mana sources

early_game: Deploy acceleration like Ancient Tomb, Sol Lands, and Chrome Mox to cast Chalice of the Void and Blood Moon by turn 1 or 2
Imprint Pyrogoyf or play Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to establish early board presence
Use The One Ring to recover cards if the opening hand is thin
Turn 1: Play Chrome Mox, sacrifice it to cast Chalice of the Void (usually on 1 or 2), a turn-1 threat (e.g., Monastery Swiftspear, Goblin Guide), or a discard spell.
Turn 2: Play a land, then cast your second threat or discard to remove a key opposing piece.
Sequence lands carefully to ensure you can cast your Mox/Chalice/threats on curve without flooding
if you have multiple Moxes, hold one for a potential turn-2 play.
mid_game: Freeze Urza's Saga at two lore counters with Blood Moon to generate Construct tokens indefinitely
Equip tokens with Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Lavaspur Boots to apply consistent pressure
Deploy graveyard hate like Soul-Guide Lantern and Pithing Needle to disrupt opponent's strategies
Treat Chrome Mox as a one-time acceleration tool—save it for when you need to cast a high-impact spell early.
Manage Chalice of the Void by targeting the opponent's most disruptive mana value (usually 1 or 2).
Use discard to remove counters, removal, or combo pieces rather than wasting it on irrelevant cards.
Keep your graveyard in mind if the opponent plays graveyard-based strategies, but prioritize board presence over graveyard recursion.
Commit threats aggressively on turns 1-3 to force interaction.
Hold back a threat or two if the opponent has shown sweepers or hard removal.
late_game: Push damage through equipped tokens and Shadowspear's lifelink to win the life total race
Maintain prison board state with Chalice and Blood Moon to stall opponent's recovery
Use sideboard tools like Disruptor Flute and Fiery Confluence to handle specific late-game threats
Win via combat damage with multiple threats, Broadside Bombardiers' triggered ability (which scales with damage dealt), or direct damage spells.
Use discard to clear the opponent's hand of blockers or removal, then commit all remaining threats to close the game.
Go all-in when the opponent has no removal, counters, or sweepers in hand or on the board, or when you can safely attack with multiple threats without risking a fatal counterattack.
Also commit when you can activate Broadside Bombardiers' ability to deal lethal damage.

key_cards.name: Urza's Saga
key_cards.notes: Frozen at two lore counters by Blood Moon to create infinite Construct tokens

key_cards.name: Blood Moon
key_cards.notes: Locks opponent's non-basic lands and freezes Urza's Saga progression

key_cards.name: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker
key_cards.notes: Imprints Pyrogoyf and equips to tokens for massive damage output

key_cards.name: Chalice of the Void
key_cards.notes: Set to 1 to lock lands or key spells, disrupting opponent's early game

key_cards.name: The One Ring
key_cards.notes: Recovers cards to compensate for thin opening hands

key_cards.name: Soul-Guide Lantern
key_cards.notes: Reduces Barrowgoyf size and disrupts Murktide Regent's Delve

key_cards.name: Lavaspur Boots
key_cards.notes: Provides flying and +1/+1 to tokens, synergizes with Fable's other side

key_cards.name: Shadowspear
key_cards.notes: Balances life total race and provides combat damage

key_cards.name: Pithing Needle
key_cards.notes: Name-drops key cards like Karn, the Great Creator or Tamiyo, Seasoned Scholar

key_cards.name: Disruptor Flute
key_cards.notes: Counters Show and Tell, Karn, and Nomads en-Kor

sequencing_tips: Play Ancient Tomb, then Lavaspur Boots, then a Mox, imprint Pyrogoyf, and follow with Chalice of the Void for 1
If Chalice is inefficient against the matchup, play Fable of the Mirror-Breaker on turn 1 instead
Against certain decks, lead with Magus of the Moon on turn 1 if you can draw additional mana sources
Use Urza's Saga's third chapter to find a Chrome Mox and fix mana
Chrome Mox is a one-shot, so sequence it carefully to maximize its impact.
Chalice of the Void can be dead if the opponent's key spells are at unexpected mana values, so be flexible with your targets.
Broadside Bombardiers scales with damage dealt, so use direct damage or discard to remove blockers before attacking.
Avoid overextending into known sweepers, and always track the opponent's hand for counters and removal.
Remember that Red-Black Stompy wins by out-sping the opponent in the early game, so prioritize speed and disruption over late-game value.

matchups.advice: Play Moon effects early if going first; use Chalice for 1 if drawing first. Soul-Guide Lantern prevents reanimation for a round. Sideboard Shadowspear for Barrowgoyf.
Use main deck graveyard hate and sideboard Pithing Needle for Tamiyo, Seasoned Scholar. Saga version handles Barrowgoyf better.
Game 1 is about drawing fewer dead cards. Post-side, use Fiery Confluence, Shadowspear, and sideboard Needle/Flute if opponent has key cards like The One Ring or Broadside Bombardiers.
Guess Chalice cost carefully. Main deck lacks answers to The One Ring. Sideboard Flutes and Needles to disrupt Karn, the Great Creator and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain.
Worst matchup. Rely on Disruptor Flute to delay the combo and Fiery Confluence to push 6 damage. Pray for luck.

Overcommit with threats to force multiple counters, use discard to remove counterspells from hand, and play Chalice on 1 to shut down their disruption. If they counter your Mox, follow up with a land and threat to maintain pressure.

Race aggressively and use Chalice on 1 or 2 to disrupt their mana acceleration. Use discard to remove ramp spells or key combo pieces. Don't let them stabilize; pressure them into mistakes before they can cast their finishers.

Use discard to remove early threats and hold back mana for spot removal if available. Prioritize blocking or trading efficiently to stabilize, then transition to a midgame pressure strategy. Use Chalice on 1 to disrupt their early game.

Apply relentless pressure with cheap threats and discard to remove key control pieces. Win before they can stabilize or find their answer cards. Use Chalice on 1 or 2 to disrupt their mana and disruption spells.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, hard removal, and sweepers.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Now, instead of killing it, Moon freezes the Saga as it is... lets us create Constructs forever

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: if you freeze it when it has two lore counters, it can still create Construct tokens... lets us create Constructs forever
Fable of the Mirror-Breaker is the best card in the game... equip the token on turn 2
Shadowspear makes the lifepoints race balanced because of its lifelink

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: I'd play Tomb, Lavaspur Boots, Mox, then imprint Pyrogoyf and follow with Chalice for 1
If Chalice isn't efficient against my opponent... I'd play Fable on turn 1
Against a few decks, the best strategy is playing Magus on turn 1, though if you do this, you'll need to draw other sources of mana
the third chapter of the Saga can always find a Chrome Mox and fix our mana for us

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: problematic cards, like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent
Karn, the Great Creator or Nomads en-Kor
Tezzeret, Cruel Captain decks became more popular, as both Forge Combo, Artifact Blue, and the new Affinity lists use Pinnacle Emissary
naturally resistant to Boseiju, Who Endures and Force of Vigor
vulnerable to Daze as well... crumble very easily if your opponent plays a Wasteland
